findings.diff_summary: Diff adds explicit REPO_ROOT capture in Step 2 bash block, removes venv activation prefix from both Step 5 python3 invocations, and passes --repo-root "${REPO_ROOT}" to execute-push.py in both invocation examples. No new verbose sections introduced.

pre_existing_advisory.note: Contains historical narrative (TTL raised from 120 to 180, task reference 20260519-211515 R4/AC4, observed latency rationale). Pre-existing. 13 lines is below the minor threshold (50 lines). Advisory only — not a new-in-diff violation.

findings.recommendation: No action required for this diff. Pre-existing Push-analyst grant TTL section (13 lines) is below the minor threshold and is advisory only.

findings.diff_summary: Python script is out of prompt-inspector scope (detection rules target ~/.claude/commands/*.md and ~/.claude/agents/*.md only). Diff adds --repo-root argument definition, chdir handling (Step 1b), and expands exit-code docs. No command/agent markdown verbosity violation.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex reviewed the draft findings and raised 3 issues and 1 observation: (1) diff summary for commands/push.md was inaccurate — the actual diff removes venv activation, adds REPO_ROOT capture, and passes --repo-root, not merely changing python3 invocation; (2) intro paragraph lines 8-20 was overflagged as verbose — lines 15-20 are substantive procedural rules (execute Steps 0-5, require analyst validation, never call push.sh directly, no user bounce); (3) the execute-push.py docstring description was inaccurate — the diff only adds --repo-root docs and chdir handling, not a full docstring rewrite; (4) OBSERVATION_ONLY: actual line counts are 233/329 not 234/330, does not affect verdict.

findings.issue: commands/push.md diff summary was inaccurate — described as 'python3 → source venv/bin/activate && python3' and 2 changed lines, but actual diff removes venv activation, adds REPO_ROOT variable capture, and passes --repo-root to execute-push.py
findings.proposed_fix: Replace with: Diff adds explicit REPO_ROOT capture in Step 2 bash block, removes venv activation from both Step 5 examples, and passes --repo-root. No new verbose sections introduced.
findings.rationale: Corrected diff description in the finding output; does not change the zero-new-violations verdict.

findings.issue: Intro paragraph lines 8-20 was overflagged as verbose. Lines 15-20 are substantive procedural rules: execute Steps 0-5, require push-analyst validation, do not call push.sh directly, do not bounce to user.
findings.proposed_fix: Reclassify as procedural, not a verbosity violation. Remove from pre-existing verbose section identification.
findings.rationale: Intro paragraph removed from findings. Lines 11-14 explain disable-model-invocation semantics (borderline contextual), but lines 15-20 are mandatory execution rules for the agent — they directly govern how the agent MUST behave. The entire paragraph is procedural. Not a violation.
